name: Zinfandel Beef Stew
recipeIngredient: 4 lb Stewing beef, cut into 2-inch cubes
4 Carrots, peeled and cut into thin slices
2 cups Hot beef stock or bouillon (homemade, canned, or reconstituted)
1 Whole tomato (canned or fresh), peeled and coarsely chopped
5 Potatoes, peeled and cut into 3-inch chunks
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak a wet clay pot in water for 15 minutes.
2. Dry the beef cubes thoroughly and sauté briefly in a large skillet over high heat. Remove meat to the pot.
3. Cook onions and carrots in the skillet for 3 minutes over medium-high heat (add an additional tablespoon of oil if necessary).
4. Pour beef stock into the skillet, add wine, and bring almost to a boil.
5. Simmer for 5 minutes.
6. Add salt, garlic, tomatoes, and pepper.
7. Place remaining herbs in a square of cheesecloth and tie into a bundle with string or thread.
8. Bury the bundle in the meat.
9. Pour contents of the skillet over beef and herbs. Cover and place in a cold oven. Set oven temperature to 450°F and cook for 90 minutes.
10. While stew is cooking, steam additional carrots and potatoes.
11. Remove casserole from the oven and discard herb bundle.
12. Strain juice into a large bowl.
13. Return beef cubes to the casserole.
14. Press baked vegetables through a sieve placed over the stewing juice.
15. Pour juice over beef cubes in the casserole.
16. Add steamed carrots and potatoes to the casserole.
17. Cover and return to oven for 15-20 minutes.
